/* ==========================================
   MODULE 1: THE DICTIONARY
   ========================================== */
function DictionaryModule() {
  const [search, setSearch] = useState("");
  const [calculatorInputs, setCalculatorInputs] = useState<Record<string, Record<string, number>>>({});

  const filteredItems = useMemo(() => {
    return DICTIONARY.filter((item) =>
      item.term.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()) ||
      item.definition.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase())
    );
  }, [search]);

  const handleCalculateChange = (itemId: string, varName: string, val: string) => {
    const num = parseFloat(val) || 0;
    setCalculatorInputs((prev) => ({
      ...prev,
      [itemId]: {
        ...prev[itemId],
        [varName]: num,
      },
    }));
  };

  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
        <div>
          <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-slate-900">The Marketing Dictionary</h2>
          <p className="text-sm text-slate-500">
            A comprehensive reference glossary of strategic metrics with interactive real-time calculators.
          </p>
        </div>
        <div className="relative w-72">
          <Search className="w-4 h-4 absolute left-3 top-3 text-slate-400" />
          <input
            type="text"
            placeholder="Search metric..."
            value={search}
            onChange={(e) => setSearch(e.target.value)}
            className="w-full pl-9 pr-4 py-2 text-sm rounded-lg border border-slate-200 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-500"
          />
        </div>
      </div>

      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
        {filteredItems.map((item) => {
          const currentInputs = calculatorInputs[item.id] || {};
          const calcVars: Record<string, number> = {};
          item.variables?.forEach((v) => {
            calcVars[v.name] = currentInputs[v.name] ?? v.default;
          });

          const calculatedValue = item.calculate ? item.calculate(calcVars) : null;

          return (
            <div
              key={item.id}
              className="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-5 shadow-sm space-y-4"
            >
              <div>
                <span className="text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-wider bg-slate-100 text-slate-600 px-2 py-1 rounded">
                  {item.category}
                </span>
                <h3 className="text-lg font-bold text-slate-950 mt-2">{item.term}</h3>
                <p className="text-sm text-slate-600 mt-1">{item.definition}</p>
              </div>

              {item.formula && (
                <div className="bg-slate-50 p-2.5 rounded text-xs font-mono text-indigo-700 border border-indigo-100">
                  {item.formula}
                </div>
              )}

              {item.variables && (
                <div className="border-t pt-4 space-y-3">
                  <h4 className="text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider text-slate-400 flex items-center gap-1">
                    <Calculator className="w-3.5 h-3.5" /> Interactive Calculator
                  </h4>
                  <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-3">
                    {item.variables.map((v) => (
                      <div key={v.name} className="space-y-1">
                        <label className="text-xs font-medium text-slate-500">{v.name}</label>
                        <input
                          type="number"
                          value={calcVars[v.name]}
                          onChange={(e) => handleCalculateChange(item.id, v.name, e.target.value)}
                          className="w-full text-sm border p-2 rounded focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-indigo-500"
                        />
                      </div>
                    ))}
                  </div>
                  {calculatedValue && (
                    <div className="bg-indigo-50 p-3 rounded-lg flex items-center justify-between border border-indigo-100">
                      <span className="text-xs font-bold text-indigo-900">Output Result:</span>
                      <span className="text-base font-extrabold text-indigo-600">{calculatedValue}</span>
                    </div>
                  )}
                </div>
              )}
            </div>
          );
        })}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

/* ==========================================
   MODULE 2: THE DATABASE
   ========================================== */
function DatabaseModule() {
  const [search, setSearch] = useState("");
  const [copiedId, setCopiedId] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const [testPayloads, setTestPayloads] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});
  const [payloadResults, setPayloadResults] = useState<Record<string, { ok: boolean; msg: string }>>({});

  const filteredSchemas = useMemo(() => {
    return SCHEMAS.filter((schema) =>
      schema.title.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()) ||
      schema.description.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase())
    );
  }, [search]);

  const copyToClipboard = (id: string, text: string) => {
    navigator.clipboard.writeText(text);
    setCopiedId(id);
    setTimeout(() => setCopiedId(null), 2000);
  };

  const handleValidatePayload = (schemaId: string, specJson: string) => {
    const raw = testPayloads[schemaId] || "";
    try {
      const parsedUser = JSON.parse(raw);
      const parsedSpec = JSON.parse(specJson);

      const userKeys = Object.keys(parsedUser);
      const specKeys = Object.keys(parsedSpec);
      const missingKeys = specKeys.filter((k) => !userKeys.includes(k));

      if (missingKeys.length > 0) {
        setPayloadResults((prev) => ({
          ...prev,
          [schemaId]: {
            ok: false,
            msg: `Payload is valid JSON but misses target spec keys: ${missingKeys.join(", ")}`,
          },
        }));
      } else {
        setPayloadResults((prev) => ({
          ...prev,
          [schemaId]: {
            ok: true,
            msg: "Payload matches specification. Validation successful.",
          },
        }));
      }
    } catch {
      setPayloadResults((prev) => ({
        ...prev,
        [schemaId]: { ok: false, msg: "Failed validation: Raw input is not valid JSON." },
      }));
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
        <div>
          <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-slate-900">The Marketing Database</h2>
          <p className="text-sm text-slate-500">
            Standard schemas, data contracts, and dynamic sandbox validators for core platform integrations.
          </p>
        </div>
        <div className="relative w-72">
          <Search className="w-4 h-4 absolute left-3 top-3 text-slate-400" />
          <input
            type="text"
            placeholder="Search schema..."
            value={search}
            onChange={(e) => setSearch(e.target.value)}
            className="w-full pl-9 pr-4 py-2 text-sm rounded-lg border border-slate-200 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-500"
          />
        </div>
      </div>

      <div className="space-y-8">
        {filteredSchemas.map((schema) => (
          <div key={schema.id} className="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-5 shadow-sm grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
            <div className="space-y-4">
              <div>
                <span className="text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-wider bg-slate-100 text-slate-600 px-2 py-1 rounded">
                  {schema.platform}
                </span>
                <h3 className="text-lg font-bold text-slate-900 mt-2">{schema.title}</h3>
                <p className="text-sm text-slate-600 mt-1">{schema.description}</p>
              </div>

              {/* Specification Code Block */}
              <div className="relative">
                <button
                  onClick={() => copyToClipboard(schema.id, schema.schema_json)}
                  className="absolute right-3 top-3 p-1.5 rounded-lg bg-slate-800 hover:bg-slate-700 text-slate-300 hover:text-white transition"
                  title="Copy schema JSON"
                >
                  {copiedId === schema.id ? <Check className="w-4 h-4 text-emerald-400" /> : <Copy className="w-4 h-4" />}
                </button>
                <pre className="p-4 bg-slate-950 text-slate-200 rounded-lg text-xs font-mono overflow-x-auto max-h-72">
                  {schema.schema_json}
                </pre>
              </div>
            </div>

            {/* Validation Sandbox Panel */}
            <div className="border-t lg:border-t-0 lg:border-l pt-6 lg:pt-0 lg:pl-6 space-y-4 flex flex-col justify-between">
              <div>
                <h4 className="text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider text-slate-400 flex items-center gap-1.5">
                  <Database className="w-4 h-4" /> Live Schema Validator
                </h4>
                <p className="text-xs text-slate-500 mt-1">
                  Paste a JSON payload below to test conformity against the primary spec keys.
                </p>
                <textarea
                  placeholder="Paste test JSON payload here..."
                  value={testPayloads[schema.id] || ""}
                  onChange={(e) =>
                    setTestPayloads((prev) => ({ ...prev, [schema.id]: e.target.value }))
                  }
                  className="w-full mt-3 h-40 p-3 bg-slate-50 text-xs font-mono rounded border border-slate-200 focus:outline-none focus:ring-1 focus:ring-indigo-500"
                />
              </div>

              <div>
                <button
                  onClick={() => handleValidatePayload(schema.id, schema.schema_json)}
                  className="w-full bg-indigo-600 text-white text-xs font-bold py-2.5 rounded-lg hover:bg-indigo-700 transition"
                >
                  Validate Schema Payload
                </button>
                {payloadResults[schema.id] && (
                  <div
                    className={`mt-3 p-3 rounded-lg text-xs flex items-start gap-2 border ${
                      payloadResults[schema.id].ok
                        ? "bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-800 border-emerald-100"
                        : "bg-rose-50 text-rose-800 border-rose-100"
                    }`}
                  >
                    {payloadResults[schema.id].ok ? (
                      <Check className="w-4 h-4 shrink-0 text-emerald-600 mt-0.5" />
                    ) : (
                      <Check className="w-4 h-4 shrink-0 text-rose-600 mt-0.5" />
                    )}
                    <span>{payloadResults[schema.id].msg}</span>
                  </div>
                )}
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

    return EVENTS.filter((e) => e.type === filterType);

  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
        <div>
          <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-slate-900">Marketing Big Events</h2>
          <p className="text-sm text-slate-500">
            A chronological and strategic record of major conferences and campaigns that shaped global marketing.
          </p>
        </div>
        <div className="flex gap-2">
          <button
            onClick={() => setFilterType("all")}
            className={`px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-bold rounded ${
              filterType === "all" ? "bg-slate-800 text-white" : "bg-slate-100 hover:bg-slate-200"
            }`}
          >
            All
          </button>
          <button
            onClick={() => setFilterType("conference")}
            className={`px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-bold rounded ${
              filterType === "conference" ? "bg-slate-800 text-white" : "bg-slate-100 hover:bg-slate-200"
            }`}
          >
            Conferences
          </button>
          <button
            onClick={() => setFilterType("campaign")}
            className={`px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-bold rounded ${
              filterType === "campaign" ? "bg-slate-800 text-white" : "bg-slate-100 hover:bg-slate-200"
            }`}
          >
            Campaigns
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
        {filteredEvents.map((e) => (
          <div
            key={e.id}
            className="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-5 shadow-sm space-y-4 relative overflow-hidden"
          >

              <div>
                <span
                  className={`text-[9px] font-bold uppercase tracking-wider px-2 py-0.5 rounded ${
                    e.type === "conference" ? "bg-indigo-50 text-indigo-700" : "bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-700"

        ))}

  );
}

/* ==========================================
   MODULE 4: THE MARP (HALL OF FAME)
   ========================================== */
function MarpModule() {
  const [selectedMarketer, setSelectedMarketer] = useState<MarketerProfile | null>(null);
  const [outreachType, setOutreachType] = useState<"email" | "linkedin">("email");
  const [customGoal, setCustomGoal] = useState("propose a high-conversion newsletter format split-test");
  const [generatedSequence, setGeneratedSequence] = useState<string | null>(null);

  const handleGenerateSequence = (profile: MarketerProfile) => {
    setSelectedMarketer(profile);
    let sequence = "";

    if (outreachType === "email") {
      sequence = `Subject: Quick query on ${profile.notable_work.split(" (")[0]} philosophy -- partnership?

Hi team,

I was reviewing the classical Direct Response framework behind Ogilvy's ${profile.notable_work.split(" (")[0]} philosophy and we are attempting to replicate this scientific discipline within modern enterprise workflows.

We are currently looking to ${customGoal}. 

Given your focus on benefit-led copy, do you have 10 minutes for a direct-response strategy exchange this Thursday?

Regards,
[Your Name]
Marketing Hub Director`;
    } else {
      sequence = `Hi, I've spent significant time analyzing your ${profile.notable_work.split(" (")[0]} campaign framework. We're currently leveraging zero-copy data warehouses to optimize direct attribution, and we'd love to ${customGoal}. I'd appreciate the chance to connect.`;
    }

    setGeneratedSequence(sequence);
  };

  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      <div>
        <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-slate-900">The Marketing Marp</h2>
        <p className="text-sm text-slate-500">
          The Hall of Fame of the greatest marketing minds. Study their philosophies and generate outbound outreach sequences inspired by their methodologies.
        </p>
      </div>

      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
        {/* Left: Marketer list */}
        <div className="lg:col-span-2 grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4">
          {MARKETERS.map((m) => (
            <div
              key={m.id}
              onClick={() => setSelectedMarketer(m)}
              className={`bg-white border p-5 rounded-xl cursor-pointer shadow-sm transition hover:border-indigo-400 flex flex-col justify-between ${
                selectedMarketer?.id === m.id ? "border-indigo-600 ring-1 ring-indigo-600" : "border-slate-200"
              }`}
            >
              <div className="space-y-2">
                <div className="flex justify-between items-start">
                  <h3 className="font-bold text-slate-900 text-base">{m.name}</h3>
                  <span className="text-xs text-slate-400">{m.era}</span>
                </div>
                <p className="text-xs text-slate-600 line-clamp-3">{m.bio}</p>
              </div>

              <div className="mt-4 border-t pt-3 flex items-center justify-between">
                <span className="text-[10px] font-semibold text-indigo-600 uppercase">
                  {m.notable_work.substring(0, 22)}...
                </span>
                <button
                  onClick={(e) => {
                    e.stopPropagation();
                    handleGenerateSequence(m);
                  }}
                  className="bg-indigo-600 text-white text-xs font-bold px-3 py-1.5 rounded hover:bg-indigo-700"
                >
                  Draft Outreach
                </button>
              </div>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>

        {/* Right: Pitch Generator panel */}
        <div className="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-5 shadow-sm space-y-4">
          <h3 className="font-bold text-sm text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-wider flex items-center gap-1">
            <Share2 className="w-4 h-4 text-indigo-500" /> Outreach Sandbox
          </h3>

          {selectedMarketer ? (
            <div className="space-y-4">
              <div className="border-b pb-3">
                <h4 className="font-extrabold text-slate-950 text-base">{selectedMarketer.name}</h4>
                <p className="text-xs text-indigo-600 font-medium mt-1">
                  Philosophy: {selectedMarketer.philosophy}
                </p>
              </div>

              {/* Configure pitches */}
              <div className="space-y-3 text-xs">
                <div>
                  <label className="font-bold text-slate-500">Outreach Channel</label>
                  <div className="flex gap-2 mt-1">
                    <button
                      onClick={() => setOutreachType("email")}
                      className={`flex-1 py-1.5 rounded font-bold border transition flex items-center justify-center gap-1 ${
                        outreachType === "email" ? "bg-indigo-50 border-indigo-200 text-indigo-700" : "bg-slate-50 border-slate-200"
                      }`}
                    >
                      <Mail className="w-3.5 h-3.5" /> Email Invite
                    </button>
                    <button
                      onClick={() => setOutreachType("linkedin")}
                      className={`flex-1 py-1.5 rounded font-bold border transition flex items-center justify-center gap-1 ${
                        outreachType === "linkedin" ? "bg-indigo-50 border-indigo-200 text-indigo-700" : "bg-slate-50 border-slate-200"
                      }`}
                    >
                      <Linkedin className="w-3.5 h-3.5" /> LinkedIn Invite
                    </button>
                  </div>
                </div>

                <div>
                  <label className="font-bold text-slate-500">Campaign / Pitch Goal</label>
                  <input
                    type="text"
                    value={customGoal}
                    onChange={(e) => setCustomGoal(e.target.value)}
                    className="w-full mt-1 border p-2 rounded text-xs bg-slate-50 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-indigo-500"
                  />
                </div>

                <button
                  onClick={() => handleGenerateSequence(selectedMarketer)}
                  className="w-full bg-indigo-600 hover:bg-indigo-700 text-white font-bold py-2 rounded-lg"
                >
                  Re-Compile Pitch
                </button>
              </div>

              {generatedSequence && (
                <div className="space-y-2 mt-4">
                  <div className="flex justify-between items-center text-xs">
                    <span className="font-bold text-slate-400 uppercase">Compiled Sequence</span>
                    <button
                      onClick={() => navigator.clipboard.writeText(generatedSequence)}
                      className="text-indigo-600 hover:text-indigo-700 font-bold"
                    >
                      Copy Content
                    </button>
                  </div>
                  <pre className="p-3 bg-slate-950 text-slate-100 rounded-lg text-xs font-mono max-h-60 overflow-y-auto whitespace-pre-wrap">
                    {generatedSequence}
                  </pre>
                </div>
              )}
            </div>
          ) : (
            <div className="h-48 flex flex-col items-center justify-center text-slate-400 text-xs">
              <User className="w-12 h-12 mb-2 stroke-1" />
              <span>Select a marketer or click "Draft Outreach" to activate the sandbox.</span>
            </div>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

/* ==========================================
   MODULE 5: THE PHONEBOOK
   ========================================== */
function PhonebookModule() {
  const [categoryFilter, setCategoryFilter] = useState<"All" | "Agency" | "Vendor" | "Tool">("All");
  const [search, setSearch] = useState("");
  const [simulatedQuotes, setSimulatedQuotes] = useState<Record<string, { status: "pending" | "resolved"; text: string }>>({});

  const filteredContacts = useMemo(() => {
    return PHONEBOOK.filter((item) => {
      const matchCategory = categoryFilter === "All" || item.category === categoryFilter;
      const matchSearch =
        item.name.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()) ||
        item.specialty.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase());
      return matchCategory && matchSearch;
    });
  }, [categoryFilter, search]);

  const handleRequestQuote = (id: string, name: string) => {
    setSimulatedQuotes((prev) => ({
      ...prev,
      [id]: { status: "pending", text: "Compiling inquiry and generating proposal..." },
    }));

    setTimeout(() => {
      setSimulatedQuotes((prev) => ({
        ...prev,
        [id]: {
          status: "resolved",
          text: `PROPOSAL GENERATED FOR: ${name}
Target Budget: Dynamic Retainer Model.
Scope of Work: Modernizing multi-channel pipelines with direct Snowflake/Redpanda data streams.
Suggested Kickoff: Next Tuesday (11:00 AM EST).
Reference ID: prop_phone_${id}`,
        },
      }));
    }, 1500);
  };

  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row md:items-center justify-between gap-4">
        <div>
          <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-slate-900">The Marketing Phonebook</h2>
          <p className="text-sm text-slate-500">
            A verified directory of elite agencies, system integration vendors, and marketing tools.
          </p>
        </div>

        <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
          <div className="relative w-60">
            <Search className="w-4 h-4 absolute left-3 top-3 text-slate-400" />
            <input
              type="text"
              placeholder="Search services..."
              value={search}
              onChange={(e) => setSearch(e.target.value)}
              className="w-full pl-9 pr-4 py-2 text-sm rounded-lg border border-slate-200 focus:ring-1 focus:ring-indigo-500"
            />
          </div>
          <select
            value={categoryFilter}
            onChange={(e) => setCategoryFilter(e.target.value as any)}
            className="border p-2 rounded-lg text-sm bg-white focus:ring-1 focus:ring-indigo-500"
          >
            <option value="All">All Categories</option>
            <option value="Agency">Agencies</option>
            <option value="Vendor">Vendors</option>
            <option value="Tool">Tools</option>
          </select>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6">
        {filteredContacts.map((contact) => (
          <div
            key={contact.id}
            className="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-5 shadow-sm flex flex-col justify-between"
          >
            <div className="space-y-3">
              <div className="flex justify-between items-start">
                <div>
                  <span className="text-[9px] font-bold uppercase tracking-wider bg-slate-100 text-slate-600 px-2 py-0.5 rounded">
                    {contact.category}
                  </span>
                  <h3 className="text-lg font-bold text-slate-900 mt-2">{contact.name}</h3>
                  <p className="text-xs text-indigo-600 font-medium">{contact.specialty}</p>
                </div>
                <div className="text-right">
                  <span className="text-sm font-extrabold text-amber-500">★ {contact.rating}</span>
                </div>
              </div>

              <p className="text-xs text-slate-500 leading-relaxed">{contact.description}</p>
              <div className="text-xs font-mono text-slate-400 bg-slate-50 p-2 rounded">
                Contact: {contact.contact}
              </div>
            </div>

            <div className="mt-5 pt-4 border-t space-y-3">
              <button
                onClick={() => handleRequestQuote(contact.id, contact.name)}
                className="w-full bg-slate-900 text-white text-xs font-bold py-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-slate-800 transition"
              >
                Inquire & Request Proposal
              </button>

              {simulatedQuotes[contact.id] && (
                <div className="p-3 bg-slate-950 text-slate-100 font-mono text-[10px] rounded-lg overflow-x-auto whitespace-pre-wrap">
                  {simulatedQuotes[contact.id].text}
                </div>
              )}
            </div>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

/* ==========================================
   MODULE 6: THE TRAINING GROUND
   ========================================== */
function TrainingModule() {
  const [scores, setScores] = useState<Record<string, number>>({});
  const [selectedAnswers, setSelectedAnswers] = useState<Record<string, number>>({});
  const [metricImpacts, setMetricImpacts] = useState({ retention: 90, cac: 50, revenue: 100 });

  const handleSelectOption = (scenarioId: string, qIdx: number, score: number, optionIdx: number) => {
    setSelectedAnswers((prev) => ({ ...prev, [scenarioId]: optionIdx }));
    setScores((prev) => ({ ...prev, [scenarioId]: score }));

    const opt = SCENARIOS.find((s) => s.id === scenarioId)?.options[optionIdx];
    if (opt) {
      setMetricImpacts((prev) => ({
        retention: Math.min(100, Math.max(0, prev.retention + opt.impact.retention)),
        cac: Math.min(150, Math.max(0, prev.cac + opt.impact.cac)),
        revenue: Math.min(250, Math.max(0, prev.revenue + opt.impact.revenue)),
      }));
    }
  };

  const currentMarketingIq = useMemo(() => {
    const vals = Object.values(scores);
    if (vals.length === 0) return 0;
    return Math.round(vals.reduce((s, v) => s + v, 0) / SCENARIOS.length);
  }, [scores]);

  return (
    <div className="space-y-6">
      <div className="flex flex-col lg:flex-row lg:items-center justify-between gap-4 border-b pb-4">
        <div>
          <h2 className="text-xl font-bold text-slate-900">The Marketing Training Ground</h2>
          <p className="text-sm text-slate-500">
            Interactive, live-fire scenario branching logic simulators. Test your crisis management and evaluate your "Strategic IQ".
          </p>
        </div>

        {/* Live Simulator Performance Console */}
        <div className="bg-slate-900 text-white rounded-xl p-4 flex items-center gap-6 shadow-md shrink-0">
          <div>
            <div className="text-[10px] font-bold text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-wider">Strategic IQ</div>
            <div className="text-3xl font-black text-indigo-400">{currentMarketingIq}%</div>
          </div>
          <div className="h-10 w-px bg-slate-700" />
          <div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-4 text-center">
            <div>
              <div className="text-[9px] font-bold text-slate-400 uppercase">Retention</div>
              <div className="text-sm font-bold text-emerald-400">{metricImpacts.retention}%</div>
            </div>
            <div>
              <div className="text-[9px] font-bold text-slate-400 uppercase">CAC Limit</div>
              <div className="text-sm font-bold text-yellow-400">${metricImpacts.cac}</div>
            </div>
            <div>
              <div className="text-[9px] font-bold text-slate-400 uppercase">Revenue</div>
              <div className="text-sm font-bold text-indigo-400">${metricImpacts.revenue}k</div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div className="space-y-8">
        {SCENARIOS.map((scen) => {
          const selectedIdx = selectedAnswers[scen.id];
          const hasSelected = selectedIdx !== undefined;

          return (
            <div key={scen.id} className="bg-white border border-slate-200 rounded-xl p-5 shadow-sm space-y-4">
              <div>
                <h3 className="text-lg font-bold text-slate-900">{scen.title}</h3>
                <p className="text-sm text-slate-600 mt-2 bg-slate-50 p-3 rounded-lg border leading-relaxed">
                  {scen.brief}
                </p>
              </div>

              <div className="space-y-3">
                {scen.options.map((opt, oIdx) => (
                  <button
                    key={oIdx}
                    disabled={hasSelected}
                    onClick={() => handleSelectOption(scen.id, oIdx, opt.score, oIdx)}
                    className={`w-full text-left p-4 rounded-lg border text-sm transition font-medium flex items-center justify-between ${
                      hasSelected
                        ? selectedIdx === oIdx
                          ? opt.score >= 80
                            ? "bg-emerald-50 border-emerald-300 text-emerald-900"
                            : "bg-rose-50 border-rose-300 text-rose-900"
                          : "bg-slate-50 border-slate-100 text-slate-400"
                        : "bg-white border-slate-200 hover:border-indigo-500 hover:bg-slate-50"
                    }`}
                  >
                    <span>{opt.text}</span>
                    {hasSelected && selectedIdx === oIdx && (
                      <span className="font-extrabold text-xs ml-4">
                        Score: {opt.score}/100
                      </span>
                    )}
                  </button>
                ))}
              </div>

              {hasSelected && (
                <div
                  className={`p-4 rounded-lg text-xs leading-relaxed border ${
                    scen.options[selectedIdx].score >= 80
                      ? "bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-800 border-emerald-100"
                      : "bg-rose-50 text-rose-800 border-rose-100"
                  }`}
                >
                  <span className="font-bold uppercase tracking-wider block mb-1">
                    Simulator Diagnostic:
                  </span>
                  {scen.options[selectedIdx].feedback}
                </div>
              )}
            </div>
          );
        })}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

Based on the logs from the session, the environment has been updated to address several critical bugs, and the core of the all-in-one marketing platform has been established.
Here is a summary of the systems currently active and verified:
1. Database and Server Recovery
Database Fix: Resolved the duplicate events table collision by explicitly adding the missing entity and entity_id columns to the database. This prevented the application startup crash (idx_events_entity missing column error) and allowed the Next.js dev server to boot safely on port 3000.

You are a Principal MarTech Systems Architect specializing in high-throughput, zero-copy distributed systems. Your task is to design and implement a new feature/module for an enterprise-grade, all-in-one marketing hub. 

The implementation must adhere strictly to three core points:
1. **Standalone Modularity (Domain-Driven Bounded Contexts)**
2. **Sub-10ms Interconnected Synchronization (Zero-Lag Event Mesh)**
3. **Enterprise-Grade Surgical Execution (Data Sovereignty, Zero-Copy, Strict Validation)**

---

### Point 1: Standalone Modularity (Departmental Isolation)
*   **No Database Coupling:** The module must run as an isolated department (microservice/bounded context). It must have its own isolated database/cache (e.g., local PostgreSQL for relational state, Redis for fast key-value lookups, or DuckDB for local in-memory queries).
*   **Direct Query Restriction:** This module is strictly forbidden from executing direct SQL joins, direct database calls, or blocking synchronous HTTP/gRPC requests to other departments' databases.
*   **Local Materialized Views:** Any external customer data required for local execution must be queried from a localized, continuously synchronized read-cache.

### Point 2: Zero-Lag Interconnected Sync (Event Mesh & Streaming)
*   **Asynchronous Message Bus:** Use a two-tier event mesh to replicate state across departments:
    *   **NATS JetStream:** For sub-millisecond, non-blocking transient control signals and orchestrations.
    *   **Redpanda / Kafka:** For high-throughput transactional event logging and historical event replays.
*   **Non-Blocking Publishers:** When this module mutates state, it must write to its local database, publish the corresponding event to the event mesh (e.g., `user.opted_out`), and immediately release its system resources without waiting for downstream acknowledgments.
*   **Streaming SQL Synchronization:** Downstream data consumers must use streaming materialized views (e.g., RisingWave or Apache Flink patterns) to update local database states under 10 milliseconds from event emission (target: p99 tail latency < 10ms).

### Point 3: Enterprise-Grade Surgical Execution & Security
*   **Zero-Copy Execution:** To protect data sovereignty, customer PII must remain in place in the client’s main data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Databricks). Any extraction must use push-down SQL queries compiled dynamically for the target warehouse.
*   **Strict Input Validation:** Every incoming JSON payload must be parsed and strictly validated against an explicit schema (using libraries like AJV for JSON Schema or Zod/Pydantic for strict typing) before processing.
*   **PII Hashing & DLP:** Any customer identifier (emails, phone numbers) used outside of the local secure warehouse environment must be hashed using Salted SHA-256. Implement Data Loss Prevention (DLP) filters to prevent raw PII leakage to external APIs.
*   **Least-Privilege RBAC:** Enforce strict Row-Level Security (RLS) matching the user’s consent state (`opt_in_consent = TRUE`) before execution.

---

### Your Deliverables
When implementing the requested feature, you must provide:
1.  **System Data Flow Diagram:** An ASCII map detailing how data moves between this module, the event mesh, and its local data cache.
2.  **Strict Data Schema/Contract (JSON/SQL):** Database tables and validation contracts, including security and compliance constraints.
3.  **High-Performance Code Implementation:** Write clean, production-ready code (preferably in Go, Rust, or strictly typed TypeScript/Next.js API patterns) illustrating:
    *   The concurrent event-listener routine.
    *   The push-down execution logic or streaming view update loop.
    *   The explicit input validation check.
4.  **Error-Handling & Self-Healing Spec:** Explicit patterns for handling API rate-limits, network degradation, and poison-pill messages (using exponential backoff and Dead Letter Queues).

---

### Feature to Build/Refine:
[INSERT YOUR SPECIFIC FEATURE OR USER STORY HERE, E.g., "Build a standalone SMS & WhatsApp transactional delivery module that triggers on abandoned checkouts, checking real-time consent before dispatching."]

data.text:
The issue: `marketing`, `sales`, `alignment` are typed as `Record<string, number>`, so `m.pipeline_value` is `number | undefined` under `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` (which is on, given the 5324 errors). The fix: replace those with explicit typed objects matching the lib's `SmarketingMetrics`. Cleanest: import the real types from `@/lib/smarketing` instead of redefining. But the lib types use `SmarketingMetrics` with explicit fields. Let me replace the `Record<string, number>` with the actual exported interface fields. 

The simplest robust fix: type `Metrics` using `SmarketingMetrics` from the lib. But SmarketingDashboard is a client component and importing from `@/lib/smarketing` would pull `db` (libsql) into the client bundle — the exact bug I fixed earlier! So I must NOT import the lib. Instead, define a precise local type with explicit fields. Let me replace the three `Record<string, number>` with explicit field lists mirroring the lib.

Let me update the `Metrics` type to use explicit fields.

The `/status` page calls `useFetch`, which internally uses `useToast` (from `@/components/useFetch` → `useToast`). The root layout for the public site must be missing the `ToastProvider`. The error "useToast must be used within ToastProvider" happens at prerender (SSR). Let me check the root layout to confirm the ToastProvider is missing, then add it.

state.title: Inspect root layout and Toast provider

data.text:
Confirmed the root cause: the public-site root layout deliberately stripped the provider stack (auth/db/scheduler/sidebar) to keep the build small, but it also dropped `ToastProvider`. The `/status` page (and any page using `useFetch`, which calls `useToast`) breaks during SSR prerender.

The cleanest, smallest fix matching the layout's stated intent: wrap `children` in `<ToastProvider>`. `ToastProvider` is a client component, and the layout is a server component — but I can render a client provider inside a server layout (Next.js allows passing children into a client component provider). Let me check how ToastProvider is exported and whether it's a client component ("use client").

data.text:
Code integrity check across all marketing projects is complete.

## Summary

| Project | Typecheck | Build/Runtime | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| **marketing-hub** | ~5300 errors (pre-existing, tree-wide; `ignoreBuildErrors:true`) | dev server boots; `/smarketing` `/email` `/api/smarketing` → **200** | ✅ Healthy — my new files are type-clean |
| **marketing-hub-public** | same pre-existing pattern | **build was FAILING** | ✅ Fixed — build now passes |
| **marketing-docs** | n/a (markdown) | — | ✅ Clean — no broken links |

## Issues found & fixed

**1. marketing-hub-public — production build broken (BLOCKING)**
`next build` failed with `useToast must be used within ToastProvider` while prerendering `/status`. The public root layout (`src/app/layout.tsx`) had deliberately stripped the provider stack to stay small but dropped `ToastProvider`, breaking every page that uses `useFetch` (which calls `useToast`).
- Added `src/components/Providers.tsx` (client wrapper around `ToastProvider`)
- Wrapped `{children}` in `<Providers>` in the layout
- Rebuilt → `BUILD_EXIT=0`, `/status` now prerenders as static.

**2. marketing-hub — my Smarketing files had TS errors**
`SmarketingDashboard.tsx` used `Record<string, number>` index types, producing `number | undefined` errors under strict indexing. Replaced with explicit typed fields mirroring `SmarketingMetrics` → my files (`smarketing`, `deal-intel.ts`) now produce **zero** typecheck errors. Also removed an unused `Badge` import from `EmailBuilder.tsx`.

## Notes
- The ~5,300 typecheck errors in both hub repos are **pre-existing** across the ~119 uncommitted changes (the trees set `ignoreBuildErrors`/`ignoreDuringBuilds: true`, so builds ignore them). They are not from my work.
- The `marketing-suite` page discussed earlier does **not exist on disk** — only the **Smarketing** feature was actually written and verified.
- `next lint` is broken in both hub repos due to an ESLint v9/v8 config incompatibility (`.eslintrc` vs `eslint.config.js`) — a tooling issue, unrelated to code integrity.

The plan:

The hub build fails during "Collecting page data" — `/api/assets/[id]` route's module-load code hits the `events` table `entity` column error. This is the same stale-schema bug, but it surfaces here because during `next build`, Next.js imports route modules to collect page data, and the module-level DB code (creating `idx_events_entity`) runs at build time against the **production** DB config (`.env.production` → likely Turso, or local).

data.text:
Let me see the broader context — is this inside a function (like `initRealtime`)? Lines 1293-1307 are within some function. The issue is the two `events` table definitions collide. The calendar `events` table (line 928) is created first in the main `migrate()`/`openDb()` flow, then this `initRealtime` block runs `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events` (no-op since calendar events exists) and then `CREATE INDEX idx_events_entity ON events(entity, entity_id)` which crashes because the calendar events table has no `entity` column.

The real root cause: there are two DIFFERENT tables both named `events`:
1. Calendar events (line 928): id, name, type, start_date, venue... (no entity)
2. Activity/event-store events (line 1295): id, workspace_id, type, actor_id, entity, entity_id, data...

These collide. The activity-event-store `events` should be a separate table (e.g., `activity_events` or `event_log`). The calendar `events` should remain `events`. But code that uses the activity event store queries `events` expecting entity — that code is in the realtime/audit system.

The cleanest minimal fix that makes the build pass without a big rename: add the `entity`/`entity_id` columns to the existing (calendar) `events` table via ALTER (defensive, try/catch), so the `CREATE INDEX idx_events_entity` succeeds. This is exactly what I did manually on the local DB — now do it in source so it runs on any DB (including Vercel's).

But wait — is this the calendar events table or the activity table that "won"? At line 928, the calendar events is created in the main schema. Then line 1295's `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events` is a no-op. So the on-disk `events` is the calendar one (which I confirmed earlier: id,name,type,...,venue). So adding `entity`/`entity_id` to the calendar events table via ALTER makes the index creation pass.

But conceptually that's wrong — the activity event store code (publishEvent uses realtime_events, not events; but the events index at 1306 references events.entity). Actually who uses `events` with entity? Let me check. The grep earlier found `src/app/api/search/route.ts:260 SELECT id, name, type, venue FROM events` (calendar) and `/api/events/route.ts` uses `realtime_events`. So the bare `events` table is used as calendar. The `idx_events_entity` index (line 1306) is created but is it ever used? It's just an index. Creating it fails because the column is missing.

So the minimal correct fix: add `entity`/`entity_id` columns to the `events` table defensively before creating the index. Since the calendar events table doesn't need them semantically, adding empty columns is harmless (no code reads events.entity). This makes the build pass on any DB.

But a cleaner fix: the activity-event-store should use a distinct table name. However, that requires renaming and updating all references. Given AGENTS.md says prefer small targeted edits and the tree is already messy, the defensive ALTER is the pragmatic, low-risk fix that unblocks deploy.

Actually — let me reconsider. The cleanest and most correct minimal fix: change line 1295's table from `events` to a distinct name like `event_log` so there's no collision, and update the index. But that requires finding all code that queries this activity `events` table. Earlier grep showed the bare `events` table is used by: search (calendar columns name/venue), events API (realtime_events), events/[id] (calendar), events/registrations (calendar). None query `events.entity`. So the activity `events` table (with entity) is essentially only defined in schema but unused by app code (realtime uses realtime_events). 

So renaming the activity table is safe-ish but risky. The defensive ALTER is safer and truly minimal. Let me go with: add a try/catch ALTER to add entity/entity_id to `events` before the index, so the index creation never crashes the build.

Let me make the schema self-healing. I'll insert the ALTER right before line 1305 (the index creation).

The build crashes during page-data collection: `/api/assets/[id]` triggers `db/index.ts` module code that runs `CREATE INDEX idx_events_entity ON events(entity, entity_id)` — but the on-disk `events` table (calendar schema) lacks `entity`. My local ALTER fix doesn't help Vercel, which uses a different/fresh DB at build time. I need a source-level self-healing fix.

Let me make the schema idempotent: add the missing columns defensively before the index creation.

The baz-empire push has been running for ~12+ minutes across attempts and still hasn't updated the remote. The repo is 1.5 GiB and the connection is slow/flaky. The push keeps either failing silently (EXIT=0 from tail) or genuinely taking forever.

Let me reconsider. The retry loop wrapper uses `if git push ... | tail -8; then` — the exit status of a pipeline is the exit status of the LAST command (tail), which always succeeds. So `PUSH_SUCCEEDED` prints even on git failure! That's why I saw EXIT=0 before but the remote didn't update. The retry loop is broken — `tail` always returns 0, so it thinks every push succeeded and breaks after attempt 1, even if git failed.

So the push likely failed quickly (network drop) but the loop reported success and exited. But pgrep says it's "STILL PUSHING"... let me check if the process is actually the git push or a stuck shell.

Actually the elapsed was 05:49 for the wrapper, and it's still running — so attempt 1's `git push` is genuinely still uploading (the wrapper is blocked on the git push pipe). It IS pushing, just very slowly.

But this is eating enormous time. The baz-empire repo is 1.5 GiB — this is a real problem. Pushing 1.5 GiB at 4 MiB/s = ~375 seconds = ~6 min minimum if connection holds, but it keeps dropping.

data.text:
Everything is confirmed. Here's the complete status:

## Deployment status — all checked

### GitHub
- **marketing-hub** → ✅ **pushed & synced**. Local HEAD `57761a5` matches remote `main` (5 commits: Smarketing feature, db schema fix, WIP checkpoint). Verified via gh API.
- **baz-empire** → ⚠️ **push blocked by repo size**. The repo is **1.5 GiB** and the upload keeps stalling/dropping over the connection. 15 commits remain unpushed (including the `marketing-hub-public` ToastProvider fix). It does **not** affect any Vercel deploy (see below), so it's safe to leave for now.

### Vercel
- **marketing-hub** (prod) → ✅ **healthy** — `https://marketing-hub-...vercel.app` returns 307 (auth redirect = up). GitHub→Vercel auto-deploy **fired on my push**: a new Preview deploy built **Ready** in 2m from `main`, proving the build (with my self-healing schema migrations) now passes on Vercel's ephemeral DB.
- **baz-marketing-site** (the public marketing site) → ✅ **healthy** — prod returns 200. Latest deploy (20h ago) is Ready; older Error deploys were superseded. **Note:** this Vercel project deploys from its *own* GitHub repo `brahimamirzerbout/baz-marketing-site` (a separate codebase, `app/` at root), **not** from `marketing-hub-public` in baz-empire. My local `marketing-hub-public` ToastProvider fix is therefore **not** the deployed source — it's a local working copy.

### Supabase
- Project `fgjxkexgopllieggucws` → ✅ **alive** (REST returns 401 = up, needs key). Core tables exist (`users`, `campaigns`, `deals`, `contacts`, `workspaces`). CLI not authenticated (no access token), so I verified via the REST API with the anon key instead.
- Important: Supabase is the **secondary** adapter (`db-adapter.ts`), used only when `SUPABASE_URL` is set. The hub's **primary** store is **libsql/Turso** (or ephemeral SQLite on Vercel), per `db/index.ts`. The `events` table being absent on Supabase is expected — Supabase has its own 4-migration schema, not the libsql one.

### Build integrity (pre-push)
- marketing-hub `next build` now **passes (exit 0)** after I fixed two schema mismatches that were breaking the build on any fresh/stale DB:
  1. `events` table missing `entity`/`entity_id` → added defensive `ALTER TABLE` migrations.
  2. `users` table missing multi-tenancy columns (`workspace_id`/`role`/`updated_at`) → added to `CREATE TABLE` + defensive ALTER.
  These were committed (`e375ef3`) and pushed, and the Vercel deploy built successfully off them.

### What I could not complete
- **baz-empire push** — the 1.5 GiB repo won't upload reliably over this connection. To push it you'd need a stable/high-bandwidth link, or repack/split the repo (e.g. `git gc --aggressive` to shrink, or remove large history via `git filter-repo`). Want me to run `git gc` to shrink it and retry, or leave baz-empire unpushed?

state.title: Find largest blobs in baz-empire history

data.text:
The baz-empire repo accidentally committed **huge toolchain/SDK files** into history: `.rustup` (190MB, 144MB), `Android/Sdk` (146MB), `.gradle`, `Downloads/stitch.zip` (94MB), `baz-corpus/books` — totaling hundreds of MB. These bloat the pack to 1.5 GiB.

`git gc --aggressive` will **not** remove these from history (gc only repacks/reconciles; objects in history stay). To actually shrink, I'd need `git filter-repo` to strip those paths from history. But that rewrites history (destructive, changes all SHAs, requires force-push).

Given the user chose "git gc + retry" (not surgery), let me first try gc + retry as requested — gc can still help by repacking into a single optimized pack (better delta compression), which may shrink the upload enough. Let me run aggressive gc.

<path>/home/uzer/marketing-hub/src/lib/smarketing.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * Smarketing — Sales + Marketing alignment.
3:  *
4:  * Fuses marketing data (campaigns, leads/contacts) with sales data (deals,
5:  * revenue, risk) into a single alignment view: a lead→deal→revenue funnel,
6:  * shared KPIs, marketing-attributed revenue, and SLA gap detection.
7:  *
8:  * Reuses the existing intelligence modules (attribution rollup, deal risk)
9:  * so it stays wired to the live SQLite data.
10:  */
11: import { db, now } from "@/lib/db";
12: import { attributionRollup } from "@/lib/attribution";
13: import { scoreAllDeals } from "@/lib/deal-intel";
14: 
15: export interface SmarketingFunnelStage {
16:   key: string;
17:   label: string;
18:   value: number;
19: }
20: 
21: export interface SmarketingCampaignRow {
22:   id: string;
23:   name: string;
24:   status: string;
25:   spent: number;
26:   conversions: number;
27:   cpl: number;
28: }
29: 
30: export interface SmarketingRiskSummary {
31:   open: number;
32:   safe: number;
33:   watch: number;
34:   atRisk: number;
35:   critical: number;
36:   top: { dealId: string; name: string; value: number; level: string; risk: number }[];
37: }
38: 
39: export interface SmarketingMetrics {
40:   generated_at: number;
41:   marketing: {
42:     campaigns_total: number;
43:     campaigns_active: number;
44:     budget_total: number;
45:     spent_total: number;
46:     impressions: number;
47:     clicks: number;
48:     conversions: number;
49:     ctr: number;
50:     cpl: number;
51:   };
52:   sales: {
53:     pipeline_value: number;
54:     won_value: number;
55:     lost_value: number;
56:     open_deals: number;
57:     won_deals: number;
58:     lost_deals: number;
59:     win_rate: number;
60:     avg_deal_size: number;
61:   };
62:   leads: {
63:     total: number;
64:     by_status: Record<string, number>;
65:     mql: number;
66:     sql: number;
67:   };
68:   alignment: {
69:     lead_to_deal_rate: number;
70:     deal_to_won_rate: number;
71:     marketing_sourced_revenue: number;
72:     marketing_roi: number;
73:     cost_per_acquisition: number;
74:     cac_payback_ratio: number;
75:     unworked_leads: number;
76:     stale_open_deals: number;
77:   };
78:   funnel: SmarketingFunnelStage[];
79:   top_campaigns: SmarketingCampaignRow[];
80:   risk: SmarketingRiskSummary;
81:   channel_attribution: {
82:     model: string;
83:     total_attributed: number;
84:     deals_attributed: number;
85:     channels: { channel: string; credit: number; share: number }[];
86:   };
87: }
88: 
89: function parseMetrics(m: unknown): { impressions: number; clicks: number; conversions: number } {
90:   if (!m) return { impressions: 0, clicks: 0, conversions: 0 };
91:   const o = typeof m === "string" ? (() => { try { return JSON.parse(m); } catch { return {}; } })() : (m as Record<string, unknown>);
92:   return {
93:     impressions: Number(o.impressions) || 0,
94:     clicks: Number(o.clicks) || 0,
95:     conversions: Number(o.conversions) || 0,
96:   };
97: }
98: 
99: export function getSmarketing(): SmarketingMetrics {
100:   const t = now();
101:   const DAY = 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24;
102: 
103:   // ── Marketing: campaigns ──
104:   const campaigns = db
105:     .prepare(`SELECT id, name, status, budget, spent, metrics FROM campaigns`)
106:     .all() as Record<string, unknown>[];
107: 
108:   let budgetTotal = 0;
109:   let spentTotal = 0;
110:   let impressions = 0;
111:   let clicks = 0;
112:   let conversions = 0;
113:   let activeCampaigns = 0;
114:   const campaignRows: SmarketingCampaignRow[] = [];
115: 
116:   for (const c of campaigns) {
117:     const spent = Number(c.spent) || 0;
118:     const budget = Number(c.budget) || 0;
119:     const m = parseMetrics(c.metrics);
120:     budgetTotal += budget;
121:     spentTotal += spent;
122:     impressions += m.impressions;
123:     clicks += m.clicks;
124:     conversions += m.conversions;
125:     if (c.status === "active" || c.status === "running") activeCampaigns++;
126:     campaignRows.push({
127:       id: String(c.id),
128:       name: String(c.name),
129:       status: String(c.status),
130:       spent,
131:       conversions: m.conversions,
132:       cpl: m.conversions > 0 ? spent / m.conversions : 0,
133:     });
134:   }
135: 
136:   // ── Leads / contacts ──
137:   const contacts = db
138:     .prepare(`SELECT id, status FROM contacts`)
139:     .all() as Record<string, unknown>[];
140:   const byStatus: Record<string, number> = {};
141:   for (const ct of contacts) {
142:     const s = String(ct.status || "unknown");
143:     byStatus[s] = (byStatus[s] || 0) + 1;
144:   }
145:   const totalContacts = contacts.length;
146:   // MQL ≈ prospect + customer + evangelist (moved past raw lead); SQL ≈ contacts with ≥1 deal
147:   const mql = totalContacts - (byStatus["lead"] || 0);
148:   const contactIdsWithDeals = new Set(
149:     (db.prepare(`SELECT DISTINCT contact_id FROM deals WHERE contact_id IS NOT NULL`).all() as Record<string, unknown>[]).map((d) => d.contact_id),
150:   );
151:   const sql = contacts.filter((ct) => contactIdsWithDeals.has(ct.id)).length;
152:   const unworkedLeads = contacts.filter(
153:     (ct) => String(ct.status) === "lead" && !contactIdsWithDeals.has(ct.id),
154:   ).length;
155: 
156:   // ── Sales: deals ──
157:   const deals = db
158:     .prepare(`SELECT id, value, stage, won_at, lost_at, updated_at, source FROM deals`)
159:     .all() as Record<string, unknown>[];
160:   const isWon = (d: Record<string, unknown>) => d.stage === "won" || d.stage === "closed-won";
161:   const isLost = (d: Record<string, unknown>) => d.stage === "lost" || d.stage === "closed-lost";
162:   const won = deals.filter(isWon);
163:   const lost = deals.filter(isLost);
164:   const open = deals.filter((d) => !isWon(d) && !isLost(d));
165:   const wonValue = won.reduce((s, d) => s + (Number(d.value) || 0), 0);
166:   const lostValue = lost.reduce((s, d) => s + (Number(d.value) || 0), 0);
167:   const pipelineValue = open.reduce((s, d) => s + (Number(d.value) || 0), 0);
168:   const decided = won.length + lost.length;
169:   const winRate = decided > 0 ? Math.round((won.length / decided) * 100) : 0;
170:   const avgDealSize = won.length > 0 ? Math.round(wonValue / won.length) : 0;
171:   const staleOpenDeals = open.filter((d) => t - (Number(d.updated_at) || 0) > 14 * DAY).length;
172: 
173:   // ── Attribution (marketing-sourced revenue) ──
174:   const attr = attributionRollup("position_based");
175:   const marketingSourcedRevenue = attr.total_attributed;
176:   const marketingRoi = spentTotal > 0 ? marketingSourcedRevenue / spentTotal : 0;
177:   const costPerAcquisition = won.length > 0 ? spentTotal / won.length : 0;
178:   const cacPaybackRatio = costPerAcquisition > 0 && avgDealSize > 0 ? avgDealSize / costPerAcquisition : 0;
179: 
180:   // ── Alignment rates ──
181:   const leadToDealRate = totalContacts > 0 ? Math.round((sql / totalContacts) * 100) : 0;
182:   const dealToWonRate = deals.length > 0 ? Math.round((won.length / deals.length) * 100) : 0;
183: 
184:   // ── Deal risk (powered by deal-intel) ──
185:   const riskScores = scoreAllDeals();
186:   let safe = 0, watch = 0, atRisk = 0, critical = 0;
187:   for (const r of riskScores) {
188:     if (r.riskLevel === "safe") safe++;
189:     else if (r.riskLevel === "watch") watch++;
190:     else if (r.riskLevel === "at-risk") atRisk++;
191:     else critical++;
192:   }
193:   const riskTop = riskScores.slice(0, 5).map((r) => {
194:     const d = deals.find((x) => x.id === r.dealId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
195:     return {
196:       dealId: r.dealId,
197:       name: d ? String(d.title) : r.dealId,
198:       value: d ? Number(d.value) || 0 : 0,
199:       level: r.riskLevel,
200:       risk: Math.round(r.risk * 100),
201:     };
202:   });
203: 
204:   // ── Funnel: Leads → MQL → SQL → Customers (won deals) ──
205:   const funnel: SmarketingFunnelStage[] = [
206:     { key: "leads", label: "Leads", value: totalContacts },
207:     { key: "mql", label: "MQL", value: mql },
208:     { key: "sql", label: "SQL (with deal)", value: sql },
209:     { key: "won", label: "Customers (won)", value: won.length },
210:   ];
211: 
212:   // ── Top campaigns by conversions ──
213:   const topCampaigns = campaignRows
214:     .sort((a, b) => b.conversions - a.conversions || b.spent - a.spent)
215:     .slice(0, 8);
216: 
217:   return {
218:     generated_at: t,
219:     marketing: {
220:       campaigns_total: campaigns.length,
221:       campaigns_active: activeCampaigns,
222:       budget_total: budgetTotal,
223:       spent_total: spentTotal,
224:       impressions,
225:       clicks,
226:       conversions,
227:       ctr: impressions > 0 ? Math.round((clicks / impressions) * 1000) / 10 : 0,
228:       cpl: conversions > 0 ? Math.round((spentTotal / conversions) * 100) / 100 : 0,
229:     },
230:     sales: {
231:       pipeline_value: pipelineValue,
232:       won_value: wonValue,
233:       lost_value: lostValue,
234:       open_deals: open.length,
235:       won_deals: won.length,
236:       lost_deals: lost.length,
237:       win_rate: winRate,
238:       avg_deal_size: avgDealSize,
239:     },
240:     leads: { total: totalContacts, by_status: byStatus, mql, sql },
241:     alignment: {
242:       lead_to_deal_rate: leadToDealRate,
243:       deal_to_won_rate: dealToWonRate,
244:       marketing_sourced_revenue: marketingSourcedRevenue,
245:       marketing_roi: Math.round(marketingRoi * 100) / 100,
246:       cost_per_acquisition: Math.round(costPerAcquisition),
247:       cac_payback_ratio: Math.round(cacPaybackRatio * 100) / 100,
248:       unworked_leads: unworkedLeads,
249:       stale_open_deals: staleOpenDeals,
250:     },
251:     funnel,
252:     top_campaigns: topCampaigns,
253:     risk: {
254:       open: open.length,
255:       safe,
256:       watch,
257:       atRisk,
258:       critical,
259:       top: riskTop,
260:     },
261:     channel_attribution: {
262:       model: attr.model,
263:       total_attributed: attr.total_attributed,
264:       deals_attributed: attr.deals_attributed,
265:       channels: attr.channel_credits.map((c) => ({
266:         channel: c.channel,
267:         credit: c.credit,
268:         share: Math.round(c.share * 1000) / 10,
269:       })),
270:     },
271:   };
272: }

(End of file - total 272 lines)
</content>

data.text:
Sabri Suby's "Sell Like Crazy" core is **"The Leak, The Gap & The Bridge"**:
1. **The Leak** — leads slipping out of the bucket (money left on the table: people who would've bought but didn't, no follow-up, nurture, retargeting, tripwires). Quantify leads not converted.
2. **The Gap** — the difference between what you're currently making and what you *could* be making if you plugged the leak (the unrealized potential: max capacity vs current). The "could-be" revenue.
3. **The Bridge** — the strategy/machine to get from current to the potential (the plan: better offer, follow-up sequences, value ladder, ascension).

Other Suby teachings: the "9-word email" reactivation, value ladder / ascension model, the "who & where" (avatar + where they hang out), follow-up fortune (majority of sales after 5+ touches), speed-to-lead, the "straight line", offer of "the thing before the thing" (free + shipping tripwire).

I'll add a `suby` object to the metrics embodying **The Leak, The Gap & The Bridge** computed from the real data:

- **The Leak**: 
  - `unconverted_leads` = leads who never became a deal = totalContacts - sql
  - `leak_rate` = % of leads lost = (totalContacts - sql) / totalContacts
  - `lost_deals_value` = lostValue (deals that leaked out of the pipeline)
  - `no_follow_up` = stale_open_deals (deals going cold = leak)
  - `estimated_leak_revenue` = the money left on the table = unconverted_leads × avgDealSize × some conversion assumption (or lostValue + a portion). Suby's point: the leak is the people who would have bought if you'd followed up. I'll compute `potential_from_leak = unconverted_leads * avgDealSize * leadToDealRate/100` — i.e., if you converted leads at your current rate you'd get X more deals worth Y. Actually a cleaner leak metric: deals that leaked = lost deals + stale deals (people who entered pipeline and slipped). 

Let me make the Leak concrete from data:
- `leaked_leads` = totalContacts - sql (never entered pipeline)
- `leaked_leads_pct` 
- `stalled_deals` = staleOpenDeals (in pipeline but going cold — a leak point)
- `lost_pipeline_value` = lostValue (deals that leaked out the bottom)

- **The Gap**:
- `current_revenue` = wonValue
- `max_capacity_revenue` = wonValue + pipelineValue (if every open deal won — optimistic ceiling) OR a Suby-style "could be making" = capacity based on lead volume. Suby's gap = what you ARE making vs what you COULD be making if you fixed the leak. 
- `the_gap` = max_capacity_revenue - wonValue (the unrealized potential)
- `gap_pct` = the gap as % of capacity

- **The Bridge**: the action plan to close the gap, generated from the data — concrete prescriptions:
  - If `unworkedLeads > 0` → "Plug the leak: nurture {unworkedLeads} untouched leads (9-word email reactivation)"
  - If `staleOpenDeals > 0` → "Speed-to-lead: re-engage {staleOpenDeals} cold open deals (follow-up sequence)"
  - If `lostValue` high → "Recapture: win-back lost deals worth ${lostValue}"
  - If `marketingRoi < X` → "Reallocate spend from weak channels to {bestChannel}"
  - "Value ladder: ascension path from {wonDeals} customers to higher tiers (avg deal {avgDealSize})"
  - The bridge items are derived from live numbers — that's the "embodiment".

I'll also add a `value_ladder` concept: tiers based on deal value distribution (entry/core/ascension) — Suby's value ladder.

Let me add a `suby` field to `SmarketingMetrics`:
```
suby: {
  leak: {
    unconverted_leads, leak_pct, stalled_deals, lost_pipeline_value, estimated_leak_value
  },
  gap: {
    current_revenue, capacity_revenue, the_gap, gap_pct, deals_to_fill_gap
  },
  bridge: { headline, actions: [{id, title, detail, impact_metric}][] },
  value_ladder: { entry:{count,value}, core:{...}, ascension:{...} }
}
```

Let me compute these from existing data I already gather. I have: totalContacts, sql, unworkedLeads, staleOpenDeals, wonValue, lostValue, pipelineValue, avgDealSize, marketingRoi, channels.

Let me add the suby computation and types. Then update the dashboard to render a "The Leak · The Gap · The Bridge" section in Suby's style.

Let me extend smarketing.ts.

For value_ladder, bucket won deals by value: entry = lowest 33%, core = middle, ascension = top 33%. I need won deal values individually. I already have `won` array. Let me compute from it.

Let me write the additions. I'll add the suby block before the return, and add the type.

estimated_leak_value: the revenue the leak represents. Suby says the leak is people who would have bought. A defensible number: lost_pipeline_value + (unconverted_leads * avgDealSize * a conservative conversion, e.g., leadToDealRate). I'll use: lostValue + unworkedLeads*avgDealSize*leadToDealRate/100. Keep it data-grounded, label it clearly as an estimate.

deals_to_fill_gap: how many avg deals to close the gap = ceil(the_gap / avgDealSize).

bridge actions: build array of objects with {id,title,detail,metric}. Generate conditionally.

Let me code it.

I'll extend the Smarketing data layer with **Sabri Suby's "Sell Like Crazy" framework: The Leak · The Gap · The Bridge**, computed from the live data, plus his value-ladder and follow-up-fortune principles. Let me update the lib first.

 * Smarketing — Sales + Marketing alignment.
 *
 * Fuses marketing data (campaigns, leads/contacts) with sales data (deals,
 * revenue, risk) into a single alignment view: a lead→deal→revenue funnel,
 * shared KPIs, marketing-attributed revenue, and SLA gap detection.
 *
 * The diagnostic frame is Sabri Suby's "Sell Like Crazy" — The Leak, The Gap
 * & The Bridge: where money is bleeding out, the unrealized potential, and
 * the machine to close it. Every figure is computed from live data.
 *
 * Reuses the existing intelligence modules (attribution rollup, deal risk)
 * so it stays wired to the live SQLite data.
 */

import { scoreAllDeals } from "@/lib/deal-intel";

Wait — but TS errors don't cause runtime 500 in Next dev (it transpiles, ignoring types). Unless there's an actual syntax error. No, `const` redeclare is a TS-only error; at runtime esbuild would error on duplicate `const` in same scope actually! data.text:
Now I see the issues clearly:

1. Line 212: `const avgDealSize = ...` (original)
2. Line 286: `const avgDealSize = ...` again — DUPLICATE. This is the runtime SyntaxError. I must remove line 286 (use the existing `avgDealSize` from line 212).

2. Line 267: `funnel[i-1].value` — funnel is `SmarketingFunnelStage[]` with required fields, so `funnel[i-1]` is fine. But the error was at 267-272 "Object is possibly 'undefined'". That's because `funnel[i - 1]` and `funnel[i]` are indexed access — with `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` on, array index access returns `T | undefined`. So `funnel[i-1].value` errors. I need to guard. Since `i` starts at 1 and goes to `funnel.length-1`, `funnel[i-1]` and `funnel[i]` are always valid, but TS doesn't know. I'll capture into consts with non-null assertion or use a for...of with index helper. Simplest: use `funnel[i - 1]!` and `funnel[i]!`. But cleaner to read `const prev = funnel[i-1]; const cur = funnel[i];` then access with `!`. Actually the cleanest: iterate with index using a while or map over pairs. Let me just add non-null assertions.

Actually I already pushed full objects into `funnelDrops` with `label: \`${funnel[i - 1].label} → ${funnel[i].label}\``. The error is on `prev = funnel[i-1].value` etc. Let me use `const prev = funnel[i - 1]!;` and `const cur = funnel[i]!;`.

3. Line 416: value_ladder label `string | undefined` — STATUS_RUNG[rung] returns `string | undefined` because Record<string,string> indexing under noUncheckedIndexedAccess gives `string | undefined`. I'll use `STATUS_RUNG[rung] ?? rung`.

=== wire.ts head (looks like a feed/ingest) ===
/**
 * The Wire — daily marketing intelligence feed.
 *
 * Seeds a curated set of evergreen marketing articles + Nova takes. In a
 * future version, the lib can fetch from RSS/Atom or scrape specific
 * sources. Today the value is in the curated seed + Nova's per-workspace
 * relevance scoring + the "save as idea" loop that keeps the marketer in
 * flow.
 */
import { db } from "@/lib/db";

export type WireCategory =
  "strategy" | "copy" | "brand" | "growth" | "product" | "ai" | "seo" | "story" | "pricing";

export interface WireArticle {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  url: string;
  source: string;
  author: string;
  published_at: number;
  category: WireCategory;
  summary: string;
  nova_take: string;
  relevance: number; // 0..100
  is_featured: number;
  is_read: number;
  is_saved: number;
  linked_idea_id?: string | null;
  created_at: number;
=== loot/ (encyclopedia ingest referenced in git log) ===
gutenberg.ts
ia.ts
indexer.ts
index.ts
manifest.ts
openlib.ts
wiki.ts
/**
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 * THE LOOT MANIFEST — Canonical marketing canon to ingest
 * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 *
 * Hand-curated list of the books, papers, masters, and terms that
 * constitute the marketing discipline. Each entry carries the source
 * identifiers (Internet Archive, Open Library, Project Gutenberg,
 * Wikipedia, DOI) needed to fetch the full text + metadata.
 *
 * "We leave nothing behind. The discipline is ours to inherit."
 *   — BAZ doctrine
 */

export type LootSource =
  | "archive_org"
  | "open_library"
  | "gutenberg"
  | "wikipedia"
  | "doi"
  | "wikidata"
  | "crossref"
  | "manual";

export type LootKind = "book" | "paper" | "master" | "term" | "article";
=== marketingDive.ts head ===
/**
 * Marketing Dive ingest + read API.
 *
 * Scrapes marketingdive.com once, caches articles + topic pages in SQLite,
 * exposes them through the Wire so the Triangle loop can use real industry
 * intelligence when scoring/positioning.
 *
 * Topics we follow (the actual vertical taxonomy from marketingdive.com):
 *   brand-strategy · creative · mobile-marketing · social-media-marketing
 *   video-marketing · agencies · cmo-corner · analytics · marketing-tech
 *   influencer-marketing
 *
 * Formats (from real article HTML):
 *   news · opinion · column · qa · sociable · campaign-trail · deep-dive · trendline
 */
import { db, now, uid } from "@/lib/db";

export const DIVE_TOPICS = [
  { id: "brand-strategy", label: "Brand Strategy" },

data.text:
I now have strong context. There's already an ingest pattern: `loot/` (encyclopedia from Internet Archive/Open Library/Gutenberg/Wikipedia), `marketingDive.ts` (scraper → SQLite → Wire), `wire.ts` (curated feed). The hub has ~90 marketing routes but clear **coverage gaps**: social, content, influencers, podcast, video, community, advocacy, referral, affiliate, PR, positioning, competitive-intelligence, outreach, webinars, marketing-ops, martech-stack, and a unified marketing dashboard.

This is a large, multi-faceted request. Before I write a plan, I need to resolve the most important scoping decision: the breadth-vs-depth tradeoff and whether external scraping is even viable in this environment. Let me check one concrete constraint (network egress for scraping) and then ask the first question.

state.title: Check creds and ingest tables

data.text:
I have enough context. Network scraping already works (`marketingDive.ts` does live `fetch()`, `loot/` ingests from Gutenberg/IA, tables exist). No GitHub client dep yet, but raw REST works.

Key findings before questions:
- **~90 marketing routes exist**, but ~18 major surfaces are **MISSING**: social, content, influencers, podcast, video, community, advocacy, referral, affiliate, PR, positioning, competitive-intelligence, outreach, webinars, marketing-ops, martech-stack, unified dashboards.
- **Ingest infra exists** (`loot/`, `marketingDive.ts`, `wire.ts`) with a clear pattern: fetch → cache in SQLite → expose via lib/Wire. Reusable.
- **The Smarketing feature is mid-edit** (the Suby framework edit is currently broken — but that's a separate implementation thread; I'll note it as a precondition).

Now the most important scoping question. The request has two parts: (1) "cover all marketing aspects in the hub" and (2) "scrape the net and GitHub for repos/resources needed." These interact: scraping feeds the content/knowledge for the new surfaces. But covering 18 missing surfaces + a scraping engine is a very large plan. I need to resolve scope and prioritization first.

"Cover all" + a scraping engine is a very large body of work. How should the plan prioritize?

state.title: Inspect existing table patterns

data.text:
116 tables, each feature gets its own dedicated table (id/name/status/created_at/updated_at pattern, JSON `metrics` blobs). The hub is firmly SQLite-centric with a self-healing migration approach (the pattern I established). So the new surfaces should follow suit: dedicated tables per surface, created via the existing `db.exec` schema block + defensive ALTERs.

Now the **scraping engine** design — the core new infra. Let me resolve its architecture: one generic engine vs. per-source adapters (the existing `loot/` already uses per-source adapters: gutenberg.ts, ia.ts, wiki.ts). I'll recommend matching that adapter pattern. Let me check how `loot` adapters are orchestrated to recommend reuse.

state.title: Inspect loot orchestration pattern

data.text:
There's even a `migrations-loot.ts` pattern for dedicated migrations. This is a strong reusable foundation: per-source adapters → indexer → SQLite tables → search index, with politeness (max 4 concurrent, 200ms delay) and caching.

I have enough to resolve the scraping-engine design. The recommended approach: mirror `loot/` — a new `ingest/` module with per-source adapters (RSS, generic web/scrape, GitHub REST) + an indexer that caches into SQLite and feeds the Wire + new surfaces. Reuse the politeness + caching conventions.

Now the last critical scoping question: **what does "scrape the net and GitHub for repos/resources" concretely mean** — what sources and what data? This determines the adapter set and whether GitHub scraping is about *marketing repos/tooling to integrate* vs. *content/resources to ingest as knowledge*. These are very different.

state.title: marketing-hub/src/lib/db/migrations-loot.ts

data.text:
Now I have the complete reusable pattern. `migrations-loot.ts` is the template: a `migrateXTables(db)` function that creates per-source tables + a shared `loot_index` (kind/ref_id/title/haystack/year/category) for cross-source search.

I have enough to design the full plan. The ingest engine will be a new `src/lib/ingest/` module mirroring `loot/`:
- `migrations-ingest.ts` → `migrateIngestTables(db)` creating `ingest_web`, `ingest_github`, `ingest_competitor`, and writing into the shared `loot_index` (reusing it as the global index).
- adapters: `rss.ts` (web publications), `web.ts` (competitive intel — landing pages, ad libraries), `github.ts` (repo search via REST).
- an `indexer.ts` orchestrator with politeness + caching, plus `/api/ingest/run` and `/api/ingest/search` routes.

The 18 missing surfaces each get: a route page, a lib module, and dedicated tables (in the main `db/index.ts` schema block + defensive ALTERs, the established pattern). Several surfaces are powered by ingest data (competitive-intel ← web scrape; podcast/video are OUT of scope per the user's answer — wait, podcast/video were NOT selected, so those two surfaces won't have a data source. I need to clarify how podcast/video get data, or whether they're in/out).

Actually — the user selected only 3 scrape targets and **excluded podcast/YouTube**. But "All 18 surfaces" is still in scope. So podcast/video/community/etc. surfaces will be built but without a live ingest source (seed/static or manual-entry driven). That's a real design tension I should confirm: for surfaces with no scrape source, do they ship as manual-entry/seed apps, or should podcast/video be dropped?

Let me ask one focused question to close this, with my recommendation.

data.text:
All 18 surfaces, with seed/manual entry for those without a scrape source. Decisions resolved:

1. **Scope**: all 18 missing surfaces + a 3-adapter ingest engine (GitHub, web competitive intel, web marketing publications).
2. **No-source surfaces**: seed/manual-entry apps now, adapter hooks for future.
3. **Data model**: SQLite, dedicated tables per surface (existing pattern), `migrateXTables(db)` migration modules.
4. **Ingest engine**: mirror `loot/` — per-source adapters + indexer + shared `loot_index`, politeness + caching, `/api/ingest/*` routes.

Let me confirm the GitHub scraping specifics (auth + what to catalog) since it's the one adapter without an existing pattern and has legal/integration implications. Let me check GitHub auth availability.
